title: Calian

The '''Calians''' were the [[Human/Legends|Human]] inhabitants of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shiva IV]], located in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]. They were muscular beings, and the majority of them were strong and courageous. They were united into the [[Calian Confederacy]] and spoke [[Calian language|Calian]]. For millennia they remained cut off and unknown both to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]].

By Galactic standards, Calians were primitive and did not understand [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]; however, they did have [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]] and [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]].

They were capable of entering [[ryastraad]], a battle madness similar to that of [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]]. The Calians reached peace with the neighboring [[T'Syriél]] only after long years of war. The Calians were attacked by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which bombarded a Calian city. The Calians ousted the Empire from Shiva IV with the help of [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]].

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Ygal_dancing.png|thumb|left|Compared with baseline Humans such as Leia Organa (left), Calians such as Ygal Delois (right) had a distinctive red tan.]]
Calians were baseline [[Human/Legends|Humans]] from the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Shiva IV]].<ref name="Databank">{{DB|character|aronpeacebringer|Peacebringer, Aron}}</ref> Most of them shared the unique racial characteristic of having tanned red skin and dark hair. However, a few Calians such as [[Jain Havero]] had blond hair and a peach-colored complexion, which made them stand among the crowd. On top of that, a vast majority of Calians were slender and muscular.<ref name="MSW53" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|He doesn't understand—and my translatacomp still hasn't enough data to do me any good.|Leia Organa, upon metting a Calian|<ref name="MSW53" />}}
[[File:Aron-dancing.jpg|thumb|Calians wore revealing, if garish outfits.]]
Contrary to many other Human cultures, the people of Shiva IV carried very little in the way of personal equipment. The Calians did not wear many layers and pieces of cloth. All males wore a type of harness that left most of their [[Breast/Legends|chest]] exposed and a knee-length loincloth attached at the waist by an ornated [[Belt/Legends|belt]]. Both sexes were fond of jewelry and ornaments. Males were clean shaven and wore their hair short, while females wore them long. Due to their isolation, the Calians did not normally speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], the most prevalent [[Language/Legends|language]] in the galaxy, using a language [[Calian language|of their own]] instead.<ref name="MSW53" />

{{Species-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Calian species was created by writer [[Chris Claremont]] and penciler [[Walter Simonson]] for a two-issue story arc of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'' Marvel]] series, made of [[Star Wars (1977) 53|''Star Wars'' (1977) 53]] and [[Star Wars (1977) 54|''Star Wars'' (1977) 54]]. Those stories were made to recycle unused art for the canceled Marvel Comics series ''John Carter, Warlord of Mars,'' which was based on [[Wikipedia:Edgar Rice Burroughs|Edgar Rice Burroughs's]] space-fantasy warlord [[Wikipedia:John Carter of Mars|John Carter of Mars]].<ref name="Comic Book Legends Revealed #237">{{WebCite|url=http://goodcomics.comicbookresources.com/2009/12/10/comic-book-legends-revealed-237|text="Comic Book Legends Revealed #237"|work=ComicBookResources.com|archivedate=20091214181040}}</ref> Because of that, the Calians share many similarities with the Red Martians from Burrough's works. Both species have a reddish copper skintone, dark hair and walk around scantily clad.
